name: frontend-responsive
description: Implement mobile-first responsive designs with fluid layouts, breakpoints, relative units, and touch-friendly interfaces that work across all device sizes. Use this skill when writing or modifying React components (.tsx, .jsx files), when implementing CSS or Tailwind responsive utilities (sm:, md:, lg:, xl: breakpoints), when working on layout components, navigation menus, grid systems, when optimizing for mobile devices, tablets, or desktop screens, when implementing media queries, when ensuring touch-friendly UI elements, or when testing cross-device compatibility.
capabilities: Generate mobile-first responsive layouts using CSS Grid and Flexbox
Apply Tailwind CSS responsive utility classes (sm:, md:, lg:, xl:, 2xl:)
Implement responsive typography that scales across breakpoints
Create touch-friendly UI elements with proper tap targets
Use relative units (rem, em, %, vh, vw) instead of fixed pixels
Test and optimize UI components for mobile, tablet, and desktop
author: DevanB
securitySummary: All 46 static findings are false positives caused by pattern-matching without semantic context. The skill is pure markdown documentation with no executable code. False positives include hash values flagged as C2/crypto, CSS units (rem, em) flagged as cryptographic references, Tailwind breakpoints (md, lg) flagged, markdown backticks flagged as shell execution, and documentation keywords flagged as reconnaissance. The existing security audit confirms: 'Pure markdown-based skill containing only instructional guidance... No executable code, network access, filesystem operations, or external command execution capabilities detected.'
valueStatement: Building responsive web interfaces that work across all device sizes is challenging without clear guidelines. This skill provides AI assistants with specific guidance on implementing mobile-first responsive designs using fluid layouts, breakpoints, and touch-friendly patterns.
limitations: Does not generate complete responsive websites from scratch
Does not provide design mockups or visual assets
Does not test designs on real devices or browsers
Assumes the AI already has access to the codebase being modified

useCases.title: Building responsive React components
useCases.description: Create or modify React components with responsive layouts that adapt seamlessly to any screen size.

useCases.title: Implementing Tailwind responsive patterns
useCases.description: Apply Tailwind CSS responsive utilities and breakpoints to style web interfaces with mobile-first approach.

useCases.title: Optimizing touch interactions
useCases.description: Design touch-friendly interfaces with proper sizing, spacing, and interactive elements for mobile users.

promptTemplates.title: Basic responsive component
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a responsive card component that displays properly on mobile, tablet, and desktop screens.

promptTemplates.title: Navigation menu
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a responsive navigation menu that shows a hamburger menu on mobile and horizontal links on desktop.

promptTemplates.title: Image handling
promptTemplates.prompt: Create responsive image handling that adapts to different screen sizes and resolutions.

promptTemplates.title: Complex dashboard
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a responsive dashboard layout with grid that shows 1 column on mobile, 2 on tablet, and 4 on desktop.

bestPractices: Start with mobile styles first and add breakpoints for larger screens using min-width approach
Use relative units (rem, em) for sizing to respect user font preferences and scaling
Ensure all interactive elements have minimum 44x44px tap targets for touch accessibility
antiPatterns: Using fixed pixel widths instead of fluid percentages or max-width constraints
Hiding content on mobile instead of adapting it for smaller screens
Forgetting to test touch targets and interactive element spacing on actual devices

faq.question: What breakpoints does this skill use?
faq.answer: The skill follows Tailwind CSS breakpoints: sm (640px), md (768px), lg (1024px), xl (1280px), and 2xl (1536px).

faq.question: Does this skill work with custom CSS instead of Tailwind?
faq.answer: Yes. The principles apply to any CSS approach. It provides guidance on media queries, flexbox, grid, and relative units.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for email templates?
faq.answer: Yes, but with limitations. Email clients have specific constraints. The skill can guide responsive patterns but email-specific CSS restrictions apply.

faq.question: Is my code data sent anywhere?
faq.answer: No. This skill only provides guidance to the AI assistant. No code, content, or user data is transmitted externally.

faq.question: Why is my responsive layout not working on mobile?
faq.answer: Common issues include: missing viewport meta tag, using fixed pixel widths, not testing on actual devices, or CSS specificity conflicts.

faq.question: How is this different from other responsive design tools?
faq.answer: This skill guides AI assistants to write responsive code correctly from the start. It is not a design tool or testing framework but ensures best practices in generated code.
